title: Exploring Strings
description: Split, join, repeat, and shuffle strings.

slides.title: Splitting and Joining
slides.content:
# explode() – String to Array

`explode(string $separator, string $string, int $limit = PHP_INT_MAX): array` splits a string by a separator into an array.

```php
<?php
$pizza = "piece1 piece2 piece3";
$pieces = explode(" ", $pizza);
print_r($pieces); // ["piece1", "piece2", "piece3"]
?>
```

# implode() – Array to String

`implode(string $separator, array $array): string` joins array elements with a separator.

```php
<?php
$arr = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"];
echo implode(", ", $arr); // "apple, banana, cherry"
?>
```

slides.title: Other Useful Functions
slides.content:
# str_split() – Split into Characters

`str_split(string $string, int $split_length = 1): array` splits a string into chunks of specified length.

```php
<?php
$arr = str_split("Hello"); // ["H", "e", "l", "l", "o"]
?>
```

# str_repeat() – Repeat String

`str_repeat(string $string, int $times): string` repeats a string.

```php
echo str_repeat("-=", 5); // -=-=-=-=-=
```

# str_shuffle() – Randomly Shuffle

`str_shuffle(string $string): string` shuffles the characters randomly.

quizData.question: What does `implode("-", ["a", "b", "c"])` return?
quizData.explanation: implode joins elements with the given separator, so "a-b-c".
